title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
How does a permanent life insurance policy work?
Meta description: A permanent life insurance policy is one of the many types of life covers available in the insurance market. Find out how it works.
Keywords: what is life insurance, whole life insurance, benefits of life insurance
Introduction: What is life insurance?
A life insurance policy is simply a kind of financial safety net for your family. It is a contract formed between the insurance policy buyer and the insurance service provider. The life cover offered by the policy is typically valid for a particular term. If the insured person passes away during this term, then the insurance provider pays out the death benefits under the plan to the nominee mentioned.
But if the insured person survives the policy term, then most life covers pay maturity benefits to the policyholder. The only exception is term insurance, which offers only death benefits.
There are many different types of life insurance policies in the insurance market today. One such kind of life cover is the permanent life insurance policy.
What is a permanent life insurance policy?
In simple terms, a permanent life insurance policy is a kind of life cover that does not expire. In other words, the life cover is ‘permanent.’ This is quite different from other types of life insurance like term plans, which only offer coverage for a specific period. Generally, permanent life insurance plans offer a life cover combined with a savings element, so you can also meet your life goals as planned.
The cover and the benefits of life insurance offered by a permanent life insurance policy generally last throughout the life of the insured person. In other words, it is permanent, which is how it gets its name. Broadly speaking, there are two main types of permanent life insurance, namely:
Whole life insurance
Universal life insurance
How does it work?
To understand the workings of permanent life insurance, let us take each kind of permanent cover and see how it works.
Whole life insurance
A whole life insurance policy, as the name indicates, is a kind of life cover that is valid till you attain the age of 99 years or 100 years. In other words, this life insurance policy will be in force throughout your life. Whole life plans offer both death benefits and maturity benefits, as the case may be and as per the product terms & conditions.
So, if the insured person passes away before they attain 99 years of age, the insurer pays death benefits to the nominee. However, if the insured survives that age, maturity benefits are paid out to them as applicable and as per product features. Both the death benefits and the maturity benefits paid out offer tax benefits. They are tax-free in the hands of the receiver, as per section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
Another important feature of whole life insurance plans is that the premium rates are guaranteed. So, when you buy the plan, the insurer takes into account various factors like your age, lifestyle habits, occupation and more, and decides on a premium rate. This premium remains the same throughout the premium payment term.
Conclusion
That sums up how permanent life insurance policies work. Keep in mind that since the benefits of life insurance under this kind of cover are permanent, the premiums charged tend to be slightly higher. However, with permanent insurance like a whole life insurance policy, you can rest assured that you are covered all through your life.
